title: 4. Muscles
text:
There are three types of muscles we need to understand in cosmetic acupuncture. The
ﬁrst group is the most general set muscles that we call skeletal muscles. On the face, the
masseter  group  of  muscles  are  prominent  skeletal  muscles.  The  second  group  is  the
expression muscles on your face. They are skeletal muscles working for the expression of
your emotions and speech. The last group is postural muscles, and they are a part of skeletal
muscles, but are usually sustaining a certain amount of tension against gravity. Those three
muscles,  the  general  skeletal  muscles,  facial  expression  muscles,  and  postural  muscles
around neck are important in cosmetic acupuncture. Let's start with the facial muscles for
expression.
Understanding the Facial muscles
To understand wrinkles, we should know more about muscles. You have lines on your
palms, as apes do, but not pigs. Those lines come from the folding of skin. The wrinkles
which we call dynamic wrinkles are deeply related to facial muscles.
Facial muscles have a very signiﬁcant difference compared to its skeletal muscle friends.
They are weirdos. Basically, skeletal muscles move the body by moving joints. That is what
regular skeletal muscles do. To move a joint, they connect two or more bones. When they
contract, two or more bones move with the joint as a pivot. But these weirdos, the facial
muscles, are not connected to bones. One side is attached to a bone, and the other side is
attached to the skin of the face. The side attached to the bone works as an anchor, and what
happens when that muscle contracts? It pulls the skin toward that anchor. It is like strings
attached to your skin, manipulating your facial expression and speech. We are the puppets of
puppet master called brain. These muscles are also called expression muscles.

Facial  Cosmetic Acupuncture
* Example:
Biceps are attached to the humerus (upper-arm) and radius (forearm). When your puppet
master (nervous system) contracts the biceps, the humerus side of attachment works as an
anchor and the radius is pulled toward the humerus. Yes! You are doing a dumbbell curl.
In contrast, the zygomaticus is attached to the zygomatic bone and to the skin on the side
of your mouth. When your ventriloquist contracts your zygomaticus, the zygomatic bone
side works as an anchor and your mouth is pulled toward the zygomatic bone, so to speak,
toward your ear. Yes, you're grinning.
Maybe, we are little less agile and have weak muscle strength compared to other animal
friends,  but  humans  are  the  best  athletes  when  it  comes  to  facial  expression.  Many
expressions can be made by combining numerous facial muscles, which demands very
complex coordination, as does an orchestra. There is a default state of the mind and also a
default state of your face. Some look angry, even when they are not. Some look grumpy, even
when they are just thinking. Strangers always ask my friend, Hana, for directions or where to
ﬁnd certain goods at the supermarket whomever she may be. We ﬁnally ﬁgured out why and
it was because they think is she smiling at them.

Facial  Cosmetic Acupuncture
These default states of facial expression are controlled by facial muscles. Some muscles
are tightened constantly. Some are slackened. They sustain those states and as beloved
Lincoln said, "Every man over forty is responsible for his face.”  Do not make a grumpy face
when you watch a random TV show. When I see my husband reading a newspaper, I often
imagine there must have been a horriﬁc incident. However, usually, it is just his face.
As a result, your default state of facial expression affects your wrinkles. Maybe, a peace
of mind is the ﬁrst thing we should get to have a beautiful face.
Treating facial muscles with acupuncture
Although we cannot control the patient's state of mind, but we can control their muscles.
We can release the tightened muscles with acupuncture or pressure. We can remove trigger
points for the muscles too. We can help them to practice a counter action, like smiling for a
grumpy face. That is muscle building for the facial muscles. It is just like our body posture. If
we spend too much time on a chair with a computer, our psoas muscles get short and the
pectoralis muscles will be tightened. We acupuncturists can release the psoas and pectoralis
and instruct the patient to practice squats to strengthen their gluteus.
As we treat tightened or weakened muscles like psoas or gluteus, we can do same thing
with your smile and grumpy muscles like zygomaticus.
Adjusting muscle tensegrity of facial muscles also has a hidden effect: Symmetry. When
